senses.gloss: to give a bit to drink at the مُقْلَة (muqla)
guggul (Commiphora mukul syn. Commiphora wightii syn. Balsamodendron mukul)
gingerbread palm (Hyphaene thebaica syn. Palma thebaica)

etymology.text:
Apparently a Dravidian borrowing, compare Telugu మొగలి (mogali), Tamil முசலி (mucali) meaning apart from other things screw pine. Passed also into Jewish Babylonian Aramaic מוקלא and Classical Syriac ܡܘܩܠܐ (muqlā) already witnessed from the 5ᵗʰ century.

The active participle of أَقَلَّ (ʔaqalla).
